task_completion.description: Create the R script and implement vote recoding logic
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an open text editor showing `/home/ga/RProjects/senate_analysis.R`. The code should contain logic to read the Voteview CSVs and recode votes (e.g., 1, 2, 3 to Yea/1; 4, 5, 6 to Nay/0; 7, 8, 9 to NA).

task_completion.description: Implement multidimensional scaling to estimate ideal points
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look in the R script for functions performing dimensionality reduction or scaling on the vote matrix (e.g., `cmdscale`, `prcomp`, `wnominate`, `smacof`, or similar) to extract at least two dimensions.

task_completion.description: Save the estimated ideal points to the specified CSV file with correct columns
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output, file explorer, or a printed table showing `/home/ga/RProjects/output/senate_ideal_points.csv` exists and contains the columns: 'icpsr', 'name', 'party_code', 'dim1', and 'dim2'.

task_completion.description: Create a scatter plot of Dim1 vs Dim2 colored by party
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an image viewer displaying `/home/ga/RProjects/output/polarization_map.png`. It must be a scatter plot with points clearly separated into two main colors representing the political parties.

task_completion.description: Include labels for Senators Manchin, Sanders, and McConnell on the plot
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the generated scatter plot (`polarization_map.png`) and verify that the text labels 'Manchin', 'Sanders', and 'McConnell' are explicitly drawn next to their respective data points.

integrity.description: The agent actually executed the R script to generate the outputs
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for terminal commands like `Rscript /home/ga/RProjects/senate_analysis.R` or execution within an R console/RStudio, rather than just creating the files manually.

integrity.description: The agent computed the ideal points rather than hardcoding them
integrity.visual_evidence: Look at the R script to ensure the ideal points are derived from the scaling function applied to the vote matrix, not written out as a static list or data frame of coordinates.

integrity.description: The plot appears to be generated by R
integrity.visual_evidence: The scatter plot should have standard R graphical styling (e.g., base R graphics or ggplot2 aesthetics) and should not look like a screenshot taken from the Voteview website or another external source.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Output paths to verify: Script at `/home/ga/RProjects/senate_analysis.R`, CSV at `/home/ga/RProjects/output/senate_ideal_points.csv`, Plot at `/home/ga/RProjects/output/polarization_map.png`. In the Voteview dataset, party codes are 100 for Democrats and 200 for Republicans. A correct scaling will show strong polarization (separation) between the two parties along Dimension 1.
